Title: MemorySmith.Agent — Emergency Stop System
Content:
Emergency stop (StopNow) bypasses the command queue and clears adapter state immediately. It is the highest-priority control path in the system.

## C# Side (AgentBackgroundService.cs ~line 1600)

SendEmergencyStop:
1. Creates ActionData with action='stop', correlationId=new Guid()
2. Calls _queue.ClearAndEnqueueAsync(stopAction, ct) — clears all pending actions AND enqueues stop
   - ClearAndEnqueueAsync: first calls stop callback (async) for any in-flight action, THEN lock-protected clear + enqueue
3. For each item in _correlatedActions:
   - Transitions Dispatched → TimedOut via CAS TryUpdate
4. Sets _actionDispatchedThisCycle = false

ClearAndEnqueueAsync contract:
- Async stop callback is called BEFORE lock to prevent deadlock (callback may need to await something)
- After callback: lock() { _queue.Clear(); _queue.Enqueue(stopAction); }
- This ordering ensures the in-flight action's cleanup runs before the queue is cleared

## Node.js Side (MineflayerAdapter/index.js handleStop)

handleStop:
1. Sets _stopRequested = true (observed by drainQueue and active handlers)
2. Clears cmdQueue via cmdQueue.length = 0
3. Stops pathfinder: bot.pathfinder.stop()
4. Clears digging: bot.dig() (if currently digging, cancels via bot.stopDigging()?)
5. Sends stopComplete event back via WebSocket
6. Calls nextAction() to resume queue processing (drainQueue will see queue is empty and wait)

## Guard Pattern in Active Handlers

All long-running handlers (dig, goto, craft, smelt) check _stopRequested periodically:
- Before each dig() call: if (_stopRequested) return sendEvent('mineAborted', ...)
- In pathfinder goto success/failure callbacks: if (_stopRequested) return sendEvent('mineAborted', ...)
- In craft loop: check between each crafting iteration

## Complete Flow

C# StopNow → WebSocket 'stop' message → Node.js handleStop:
1. C#: _queue.ClearAndEnqueueAsync → clears all pending actions
2. C#: CAS-transitions all Dispatched correlations to TimedOut
3. Node.js: cmdQueue cleared, pathfinder stopped
4. In-flight dig/goto cancelled → emits mineAborted or similar
5. Node.js: sends stopComplete event
6. C#: ProcessEventsAsync receives stopComplete → logs acknowledgment
7. Next dispatch cycle: _queue is empty, _currentGoal may still be set → agent can be re-directed

## Key Difference from Normal Stop

- Normal stop: queue drains naturally, in-flight action completes
- Emergency stop: queue cleared immediately, in-flight action cancelled mid-execution
- After emergency stop: agent is idle but connected — ready for new commands

## Usage

- REST endpoint POST /api/agent/stop?emergency=true calls SendEmergencyStop
- Can also be triggered from dashboard stop button
- Not part of normal recovery flow — use for user-initiated halt or critical error
